prevalence.value: no endemic transmission; imported cases are rare
prevalence.trend: stable low; risk linked to travel to endemic regions

burden.mortalityRate: very low; severe disease possible in unvaccinated travelers

riskFactors: travel to endemic countries without vaccination
delayed recognition of hemorrhagic fever syndromes in returning travelers
vulnerablePopulations: older travelers and immunocompromised travelers (vaccine suitability issues)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical suspicion in febrile jaundice after travel; confirmation by PCR/serology; notification per national/ECDC guidance

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: travel clinic vaccination common; no screening in general population
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Prevention via vaccination; supportive care for severe cases

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Strong travel medicine and reference lab networks for viral hemorrhagic fevers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care; vaccination for travelers to endemic areas per WHO guidance
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Maintaining vaccine uptake and awareness among travelers and clinicians outside travel clinics
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide availability of travel clinics and vaccination services across many member states
